name: RSA Key Generation
description: Step-by-step RSA public/private key generation: choose primes p and q, compute modulus n and Euler's totient φ(n), select public exponent e, and derive the private exponent d via the Extended Euclidean Algorithm.
intuition: RSA security rests on the difficulty of factoring n = p·q. Knowing p and q lets you compute φ(n) and invert e mod φ(n) to get d — but without the primes, inverting e is computationally infeasible for large n.
